sociology.accuracyHint: Fully correct answers ÷ all attempts (partial answers count as not fully correct).
sociology.questionsTouched: {{count}} questions with at least one attempt
sociology.noWeakSubtopics: No subtopics to review yet — every sociology practice answer so far has been fully correct.
sociology.heatmapDescription: Each square is a calendar day: intensity reflects sociology practice answers plus topics you marked as read in the sociology study guide. Interview prep is tracked on a separate dashboard.
sociology.activeTimeDisclaimer: Counts seconds with Sociology Practice, Study guide, or Plan open in the foreground while this tab is visible. Interview prep time is tracked separately. This is not a measure of mastery.
sociology.studyCatalogEditsHint: You can fix typos, answers, or explanations per question below. Changes are saved only in this browser (they override the bundled JSON).
sociology.editCloseBackdrop: Close editor (discard unsaved changes in the form)
sociology.validate.TOO_FEW_OPTIONS: Add at least two answer options.
sociology.validate.NO_CORRECT: Mark at least one option as correct.
sociology.validate.CORRECT_OUT_OF_RANGE: A correct index points outside the options list.
sociology.validate.SINGLE_REQUIRES_ONE_CORRECT: Single-choice questions need exactly one correct option.

heatmap.heading: Activity

aboutPage.title: About the project
aboutPage.name1: The name “Karkas” comes from the idea of a foundation — a structure or framework on which everything else is built.
aboutPage.name2: In the context of interviews, it represents the core structure of an answer: once the foundation is clear, the details can be added naturally and confidently.
aboutPage.privacyNotice: Your practice data is stored in your browser only. Nothing is sent to a server — your progress stays private and visible only to you.
aboutPage.purpose1: Karkas is a browser-based trainer with two independent tracks: Interview prep (JavaScript, Angular, and RxJS) and Sociology. Each track has its own questions, progress, and daily plan.
aboutPage.purpose2: Interview prep focuses on open-ended technical questions: you rehearse answers out loud or in your head, compare with layered model answers, and rate yourself so practice and review adapt to weak spots.
aboutPage.purpose3: The Sociology track uses single- and multiple-choice questions with explanations after each attempt, plus a similar plan → study → practice → progress loop so you can prepare for exams in a structured way.
aboutPage.tracksLead: The home page is a simple subject picker. Pick Interview prep to open the JavaScript / Angular / RxJS flow, or pick Sociology to open that catalog. You can switch any time by tapping the Karkas logo to return home.
aboutPage.tracksInterview: Interview prep includes optional “My questions” for your own entries, a study guide with code examples and read-more links where available, and a banner that can suggest topics to revisit after weaker practice days.
aboutPage.tracksSociology: Sociology keeps its own question bank, activity by day, and spaced review dates per question (correct, partial, and wrong answers schedule the next time a question is due). It does not share ratings or topic lists with the interview catalog.
aboutPage.navHeading: What you see in the menu
aboutPage.navBody: While you browse one track, the header menu shows only that track’s links so the list stays short and relevant. On the home picker and on this About page, both sections appear together. About is linked at the top of each group so you can open it from either track.
aboutPage.howIntro: Both tracks follow the same broad rhythm (plan, study, practice, progress), but the screens behave differently where it matters:
aboutPage.step0Desc: On the interview Plan page, pick subtopics for today. After you read them in the Study guide, mark each topic as studied. Practice can stay limited to those topics until you change the plan.
aboutPage.step1Desc: Browse questions by category. Each entry includes weak, technical, and interview-style model answers (and code or links when available) so you know what a strong spoken answer sounds like.
aboutPage.step2Desc: In Practice you get an open-ended prompt and a timer. Answer as in a real interview, reveal the model answers, then move on.
aboutPage.step3Desc: Rate how it went: didn’t know, partial, or nailed it. That shapes what you see next and when each question returns for spaced review.
aboutPage.step4Desc: Progress highlights weak topics, shows an activity heatmap of recent days, and uses your ratings so harder items come back sooner than easy ones.
aboutPage.socStep0Desc: Choose sociology subtopics for the day. The plan works with Study and Practice so you can focus on a manageable slice of the catalog.
aboutPage.socStep1Desc: Open grouped topics, read the material, and try inline checks. Questions may require one correct option or several; after submitting you see whether you were right, partially right, or wrong, with an explanation.
aboutPage.socStep2Desc: Run practice sessions of multiple-choice items. Outcomes feed the same kind of spaced scheduling as interview prep so due questions surface first.
aboutPage.socStep3Desc: Sociology Progress shows accuracy, questions touched, weak subtopics, approximate learning time while the relevant pages are in the foreground, and a heatmap by day—using sociology-only data.
aboutPage.data1: Everything stays in your browser (local storage) under the shared app prefix. Interview prep and Sociology use separate keys (for example progress, activity, and plan for each track), so one track never overwrites the other. Nothing is uploaded or synced to the cloud.
aboutPage.data2: If you use another device or browser, that copy of the app will not have your history. Clearing site data for this origin removes stored progress for both tracks.
aboutPage.tagline: Train the habit of turning knowledge into answers—spoken in interviews or chosen with confidence on an exam.

quiz.loadError: Could not load questions. Check that assets are available and try again.
quiz.fallbackHintDone: No questions were due for review, so all questions were shown.
quiz.fallbackHintActive: Nothing due right now — practicing from the full set.
quiz.sessionStackAria: Subtopics included in this practice session
quiz.planFocusHint: You have topics selected for today but none marked as studied yet. After you mark topics in the Study guide, Practice will use only those questions. Until then, you still practice from the full set (or due reviews).
quiz.practicingFullBankHint: Full bank mode: every question in the list is included, not only items due for spaced review.
quiz.fallbackHintTodaysTopics: Nothing in your studied topics is due for review yet — practicing every question in those topics for now.
quiz.fallbackHintDoneTodaysTopics: No questions in your studied topics were due for review, so this round used all questions in those topics.
quiz.planTopicsCoveredBody: Nothing in the topics you studied today is due for spaced review right now. You can keep practicing every question in those topics, or switch to the full question bank (all topics).

dashboard.title: Interview Progress
dashboard.accuracyInfoToggle: Show or hide how Accuracy is calculated
dashboard.accuracyInfo: Accuracy is the share of your practice self-ratings that you marked “Nailed it”, out of every rating you gave (Nailed, Partial, or Didn’t know). Formula: Nailed ÷ (Nailed + Partial + Didn’t know) × 100. Each time you submit a rating in Practice counts.
dashboard.confidenceInfoToggle: Show or hide how Confidence is calculated
dashboard.confidenceInfo: Confidence uses the same ratings as Accuracy, but “Partial” counts as half a point: (Nailed + ½ × Partial) ÷ (Nailed + Partial + Didn’t know) × 100. It rewards almost-right answers more than Accuracy does.
dashboard.weakHint: No weak spots detected yet (need at least 3 attempts on a topic below 60% nailed).
dashboard.activeTimeProgressLabel: Illustrative progress toward 10,000 h (popular anecdote, not a goal)
dashboard.activeTimeDisclaimer: Counts seconds with Practice, Study guide, or Plan open while this browser tab is in the foreground. Background tabs are not counted. Time with the tab visible but idle still counts. This is not a measure of mastery.
dashboard.activeTimeTenKHint: The “10,000 hours” idea is a simplified popular story about expertise — use this tally as motivation, not a scientific benchmark.
dashboard.practiceRatingsIntro: How you rated answers after each question (Nailed it, Partial, Didn’t know). If you repeat the same question the same day, only your best rating that day is counted in the daily and “all days” totals.
dashboard.practiceRatingsAllDays: All days (best per question per day)
dashboard.practiceRatingsAllDaysHint: Each calendar day, each question counts once — as your strongest rating that day — then summed over every stored day.
dashboard.practiceRatingsEveryAttemptHint: Every time you submitted a rating in Practice, including repeats the same day. Same numbers as used for Accuracy and Confidence above.
dashboard.practiceRatingsEmptyDay: No practice ratings recorded yet today.
dashboard.practiceRatingsEmptyAllDays: No per-day ratings stored yet. New practice sessions will fill this in.
